name: Fried Gyoza
recipeIngredient: 6 oz Ground pork or beef (or combination of both)
1 tsp Mirin (Japanese rice wine) or sherry
1 Dried black mushroom, soaked in 2 tbsp water
1 small Pkg Gyoza skins (available at Oriental food stores)
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------
Dunking Sauce:
1/4 c Japanese soy sauce
1 ts Rice wine vinegar
1 ds Rayu or sesame oil
Pot Stickers:
1. Cook cabbage in a small amount of boiling salted water until tender. Squeeze out all liquid and mince fine.
2. Chop mushroom. Mix soy sauce, sesame oil, Mirin, pork, green onion, ginger, mushroom, and cabbage.
3. Refrigerate for 1 hour or more.
4. Place a scant teaspoon of mixture on each gyoza skin.
5. Moisten edges with cornstarch and water, fold over and seal. Crimp edges with a fork.
6. Cover bottom of a large non-stick skillet (electric is good) with oil. Brown the gyoza over medium heat (350 degrees F), turning frequently.
7. Add 1/4 cup water to skillet. Let, cover and steam on low heat for 7 minutes. Stir often to prevent sticking.
8. Remove cover, raise heat and cook for 2 minutes until crisp.
Pot Stickers are usually served as a first course. They are also excellent as an appetizer.
Suggestion: Egg Drop Soup and Pot Stickers, followed by Beef and Oyster Sauce with a crisp green vegetable and hot Chinese tea.
